# GitHub Copilot Instructions

<!--
This file is automatically generated.
Don't manually edit this file.
Instead, update the individual instruction files.
-->

## General Instructions

Store all knowledge about this project in file `docs/memory.md` so that whenever I ask you, you can use this file as a reference to understand the project and provide better suggestions.

Whenever there is any change in the project, do the followings

- Update file `docs/memory.md` with the changes made.
- Log all changes in the project in the changelog file at `CHANGELOG.md`. Use file `docs/CHANGELOG.sample.md` as a reference.
- Update all test cases in the project if needed.

File `scripts/ap_init.sh` is reserved for my external use only. Do not modify it.

Execute all shell commands in foreground mode.

Before executing any shell commands, change current directory to project root directory.


## Coding Instructions

All source codes must be written with below mindset

-   Modular & reusable
-   Maintainable (easy to debug)
-   Testable
-   Extensible


## Shell Scripting Instructions

- Use `bash` for writing scripts.
- Put all script files in the `scripts` directory.
- Script name must be prefixed by `ap_`.
- Use underscore `_` to separate words in the script name.

- The shebang line must be `#!/usr/bin/env bash`.
- All variables must have a prefix `ap_`.
- All exported variables or constants must be UPPERCASE and have a prefix `AP_`.
- Use `"${VAR}"` instead of `$VAR` for variables.


Do not need to write any tests at this moment. But please make sure that the code is testable and easy to write tests for in the future.
